GEN 1:1.text: ASV (1901) was a revision of the King James Version by American scholars. It uses more literal translation principles.
'In the beginning God created' - ASV preserves Hebrew word order more closely than KJV in many places.
'God' here is Hebrew 'Elohim', a plural noun used with singular verbs, suggesting the Trinity. ASV capitalizes 'God' as a proper noun.

GEN 1:2.text: ASV 'waste and void' is a more literal rendering of Hebrew 'tohu wabohu' than KJV 'without form, and void'.
'Spirit of God' - ASV uses 'Spirit' consistently where KJV sometimes uses 'spirit' or 'Spirit'.
'the deep' is Hebrew 'tehom', related to the Babylonian 'Tiamat'. ASV uses 'deep' rather than 'abyss' to avoid mythological connotations.

GEN 1:3.text: 'Let there be light' - Hebrew 'yehi or', literally 'be light'. ASV follows the Hebrew word order.
Light created before the sun (day 4), showing God is the source of all light.

GEN 1:26.text: 'Let us make' - The plural pronoun may indicate the Trinity, angels, or plural of majesty. ASV preserves the plural without interpretation.
'image' is Hebrew 'tselem', meaning representation or likeness. ASV distinguishes 'image' from 'likeness' in verse 27.
'man' is Hebrew 'adam', related to 'adamah' (ground). ASV uses 'man' as generic humanity, not just the individual Adam.

GEN 2:7.text: 'formed' is Hebrew 'yatsar', used for a potter shaping clay. ASV emphasizes God's hands-on creation.
'breath of life' is Hebrew 'nishmat chayyim'. ASV distinguishes this from 'spirit' (ruach) used elsewhere.
'living soul' - Hebrew 'nephesh chayyah'. ASV uses 'soul' here, though 'nephesh' also means creature or being.

GEN 3:15.text: 'bruise' - Hebrew 'shuf', to crush or strike at. ASV uses 'bruise' for both the serpent and the seed, though the Hebrew suggests different intensities.
'seed' is Hebrew 'zera', singular collective. ASV preserves the singular, pointing to Christ as the ultimate seed.
This verse is called the 'Protoevangelium' - the first gospel promise. ASV translators recognized its messianic significance.

GEN 12:1.text: 'Get thee out' - Hebrew 'lech lecha', literally 'go for yourself' or 'go to yourself'. ASV captures the command and purpose.
Abram was 75 years old when he left Haran (v. 4). ASV narrative shows God's call comes regardless of age.

GEN 15:6.text: 'believed' - Hebrew 'he'emin', from root 'aman' (firm, faithful). ASV 'believed' captures the sense of trust and reliance.
'counted it to him for righteousness' - Hebrew 'chashab', to reckon or impute. ASV uses 'counted' for legal/accounting terminology.
Quoted by Paul in Romans 4:3, Galatians 3:6, and James 2:23 as foundational for justification by faith.

EXO 3:14.text: 'I AM THAT I AM' - Hebrew 'Ehyeh asher ehyeh', the self-existent, eternal God. ASV preserves the first person, connecting to the divine name YHWH (He is).
'I AM' becomes a title for Christ in John's Gospel (John 8:58). ASV uses small caps LORD for YHWH elsewhere.
The name indicates God's self-existence and eternal nature. ASV captures this with the timeless 'I AM'.

EXO 20:3.text: First Commandment: 'Thou shalt have no other gods before me.' ASV 'before me' means 'in my presence' or 'besides me'.
'before me' is Hebrew 'al panay', literally 'upon my face'. ASV captures the sense of exclusive worship.

EXO 20:7.text: Third Commandment: Prohibits taking God's name in vain or for false purposes.
'vain' is Hebrew 'shav', emptiness, falsehood, or worthlessness. ASV captures the prohibition against empty use.

PSA 23:1.text: 'LORD' in ASV small caps represents the divine name YHWH (Yahweh/Jehovah), distinguishing it from 'Lord' (Adonai).
'I shall not want' - Hebrew 'lo echsar', I will not lack. ASV 'want' means 'lack' in Elizabethan English.
David wrote this psalm based on his experience as a shepherd boy (1 Samuel 16:11).

PSA 23:4.text: 'valley of the shadow of death' - Hebrew 'gey tsalmavet', valley of deep darkness. ASV 'shadow of death' captures both meanings.
'thy rod and thy staff' - The rod for protection, the staff for guidance. ASV preserves the shepherd's tools.
'comfort' is Hebrew 'nacham', to console or have compassion. ASV emphasizes divine comfort in danger.

PSA 51:10.text: 'Create' is Hebrew 'bara', used only for divine creation. ASV preserves this strong term for spiritual renewal.
'right spirit' - Hebrew 'ruach nachon', a steadfast or established spirit. ASV 'right' means 'correct' or 'proper'.
David wrote this psalm after his sin with Bathsheba (2 Samuel 12). ASV preserves the personal confession.

PSA 119:11.text: 'hid' is Hebrew 'tsaphan', to treasure or store up. ASV captures the sense of valuing and protecting God's Word.
'heart' in Hebrew includes mind, will, and emotions. ASV 'heart' encompasses the whole inner being.
This acrostic psalm has 22 sections for each letter of the Hebrew alphabet. ASV preserves the structure.

ISA 7:14.text: 'virgin' translates Hebrew 'almah', a young woman of marriageable age. ASV uses 'virgin' based on context and LXX translation.
'Immanuel' means 'God with us'. ASV preserves the Hebrew name as a title of the Messiah.
Quoted in Matthew 1:23 as fulfilled in Jesus' birth. ASV Matthew explicitly connects this to Christ.

ISA 9:6.text: 'Wonderful, Counsellor' - Could be read as two titles or one: 'Wonderful Counsellor'. ASV follows Hebrew punctuation.
'mighty God' is Hebrew 'El Gibbor', Mighty God. ASV capitalizes 'God', identifying the child as divine.
'Everlasting Father' - Hebrew 'Avi Ad', Father of Eternity or Eternal Father. ASV emphasizes the messianic title.

ISA 53:5.text: 'wounded' is Hebrew 'chalal', pierced or profaned. ASV captures the violence done to the Servant.
'stripes' is Hebrew 'chabburah', bruises or welts. ASV emphasizes the physical suffering.
This chapter is the great Messianic prophecy of the suffering Servant. ASV preserves its application to Christ.

MAT 1:23.text: ASV follows Isaiah 7:14 closely: 'they shall call his name Emmanuel'. The fulfillment formula is characteristic of Matthew.
'Emmanuel' is Greek 'Emmanuel', from Hebrew 'Immanu El'. ASV preserves the Hebrew form of the name.

MAT 5:3.text: 'Blessed' is Greek 'makarios', happy or fortunate. ASV 'blessed' captures the divine favor.
'poor in spirit' is Greek 'ptochos to pneumati', spiritually destitute. ASV emphasizes spiritual poverty.

MAT 5:44.text: 'Love your enemies' - Greek 'agapao', self-sacrificing love. ASV distinguishes this from 'phileo' (friendship love).
ASV 'pray for them which despitefully use you' captures the Greek 'epeRXomai', to persecute or harass.

MAT 6:9.text: 'Our Father' - Greek 'Pater hemon'. ASV preserves the familial intimacy of Jesus' teaching on prayer.
'Hallowed be thy name' - Greek 'hagiastheto to onoma sou'. ASV uses 'hallowed' (made holy) rather than 'sanctified'.

MAT 6:33.text: 'Seek ye first' - Greek 'zeteite proton', seek first. ASV emphasizes priority: God's kingdom before material needs.
'righteousness' is Greek 'dikaiosyne', right standing with God. ASV distinguishes this from works-righteousness.

MAT 11:28.text: 'Come unto me' - Greek 'deute pros me', come here to me. ASV captures the invitation to approach Jesus.
'heavy laden' is Greek 'pephortismenoi', loaded down. ASV emphasizes the burden of religious legalism.
'rest' is Greek 'anapausis', cessation from labor and refreshment. ASV captures both meanings.

MAT 28:19.text: 'in the name' is singular in Greek, indicating one God. ASV preserves the Trinitarian formula.
'Father, Son, Holy Ghost' - The Great Commission in Trinitarian form. ASV preserves this foundational formula.

JHN 1:1.text: 'In the beginning' - Greek 'en arche', echoing Genesis 1:1. ASV emphasizes Christ's eternal existence.
'Word' is Greek 'Logos', divine reason or expression. ASV preserves this philosophical and theological term.
'the Word was God' - Greek 'theos en ho logos'. ASV preserves the word order: 'God' is the predicate, emphasizing deity.

JHN 1:14.text: 'made flesh' - Greek 'sarx egeneto', became flesh. ASV emphasizes the incarnation.
'dwelt' is Greek 'eskenosen', tabernacled or pitched tent. ASV captures the imagery of God's presence.
'full of grace and truth' - Greek 'charis kai aletheia', covenant love and faithfulness. ASV preserves the Hebrew concept.

JHN 3:3.text: 'born again' - Greek 'gennethe anothen', born from above. ASV captures both meanings: again and from above.
'cannot see' is Greek 'ou dunatai idein', is not able to see. ASV emphasizes inability without new birth.

JHN 3:16.text: 'so loved' - Greek 'houtos egapesen', loved in this manner. ASV emphasizes the extent of God's love.
'only begotten' is Greek 'monogenes', one and only, unique. ASV emphasizes Christ's unique sonship.
'whosoever' is Greek 'pas ho pisteuon', everyone believing. ASV emphasizes the universal offer.

JHN 14:6.text: 'I am the way' - Greek 'ego eimi he hodos'. ASV preserves the definite article: Jesus is THE way, not a way.
'no man cometh unto the Father, but by me' - Exclusive claim. ASV preserves the necessity of Christ for salvation.

ROM 3:23.text: 'all have sinned' - Greek 'pantes hemarton', all sinned. ASV emphasizes universal sinfulness.
'come short' is Greek 'hysterountai', fall short or lack. ASV uses an accounting term: falling short of the standard.

ROM 5:8.text: 'commendeth' is Greek 'sunistesin', demonstrates or proves. ASV emphasizes God demonstrating His love.
'while we were yet sinners' - Greek 'eti hamartolon onton hemon'. ASV emphasizes the timing: Christ died for us while we were still sinners.

ROM 6:23.text: 'wages' is Greek 'opsonia', soldier's pay. ASV uses a military metaphor: sin pays death as wages.
'gift' is Greek 'charisma', free gift of grace. ASV contrasts earned wages with free grace.

ROM 8:28.text: 'work together' is Greek 'synergei', synergy. ASV emphasizes God causing all things to cooperate for good.
'love God' and 'called according to his purpose' - The promise is for believers, not all people universally.

ROM 10:9.text: 'confess with thy mouth' - Greek 'homologeseis to stomati sou'. ASV emphasizes public confession.
'raised from the dead' - The resurrection is essential to saving faith. ASV preserves this requirement.

ROM 10:13.text: 'whosoever' is Greek 'pas gar', for everyone. ASV emphasizes the universal invitation.
'call upon' is Greek 'epikalesetai', invoke or appeal to. ASV captures the sense of calling for help.
Quotes Joel 2:32. ASV preserves the Old Testament connection.

1CO 13:4-8.text: 'Charity' is Greek 'agape' - self-sacrificing, unconditional love. Distinguished from 'phileo' (friendship).
Paul lists 15 characteristics of love. Love is active, patient, kind, humble, and enduring.

EPH 2:8.text: 'by grace have ye been saved' - Greek perfect tense: 'you have been and continue to be saved.'
'through faith' - Faith is the channel, not the cause. Grace is the cause of salvation.

PHP 4:13.text: 'I can do all things in him that strengtheneth me' - Context shows Paul means contentment, not unlimited power.
'strengtheneth' is Greek 'endynamounti' - infuses with power. Christ gives strength for every circumstance.

HEB 4:12.text: 'living, and active' - Greek 'zon kai energes'. God's Word is alive and powerful.
'sharper than any two-edged sword' - The Word penetrates deeply, discerning thoughts and intentions.

1JN 1:9.text: 'confess' is Greek 'homologomen' - agree with, acknowledge. We agree with God about our sin.
'faithful and righteous' - God forgives because He is faithful to His promise and just because Christ paid for sin.
